abort.already_configured: Solaredge PV Export Limiter is already configured.
abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Solaredge PV Export Limiter instance is supported.

error.must_be_number_entity: Must be a number entity (writable inverter limit).
error.import_export_same: Import and export sensors must be different entities.

user.title: Welcome to Solaredge PV Export Limiter
user.description:
This wizard will guide you through linking your SolarEdge inverter and P1 smart meter so the integration can dynamically limit feed-in to the grid.

Before continuing, make sure:
- The SolarEdge Modbus Multi integration is installed and reading data
- Modbus TCP is enabled on your inverter (SetApp → Site Communication → Modbus)
- Your P1 / DSMR smart meter shows live import/export power

inverter.title: SolarEdge inverter sensors
inverter.description: Pick the sensor showing your inverter's current AC output, and the writable number entity that controls the active power limit.

grid_meter.title: P1 smart meter
grid_meter.description: Select the sensors that show your grid import (consumption) and grid export (production) power.

inverter_params.title: Inverter parameters
inverter_params.description: Tune the controller. Lower update intervals respond faster but write more often to the inverter. Default values work well for most setups.

data.hysteresis_pct: Hysteresis (% delta to trigger a write)

optional.title: Optional protections
optional.description: Enable voltage protection if your grid voltage occasionally exceeds 250 V (causes inverter trips). Enable tariff awareness to automatically switch to 'Negative price' mode when EPEX prices are negative. Both are optional.

setpoints.title: Mode setpoints
setpoints.description: Set the maximum feed-in (W) for each operating mode. The limiter will keep grid export at or below the active mode's setpoint.

finish.title: Almost done
finish.description: Optionally pick a notify target for failsafe alerts (e.g. mobile_app_yourphone). Leave empty to skip.

init.title: Sensors — step 1 of 2
init.description: Verify or change the sensors. After saving, you continue to setpoints and parameters.

data.grid_import_entity: Grid import — power consumption from grid
data.grid_export_entity: Grid export — power returned to grid

data_description.inverter_ac_power_entity: Current AC output of your SolarEdge inverter in Watts. In SolarEdge Modbus Multi this is called 'AC Power' (e.g. sensor.solaredge_multi_ac_power).
data_description.inverter_limit_entity: The writable number entity that sets the inverter output limit in %. Must start with 'number.' — in SolarEdge Modbus Multi this is 'Active Power Limit' (e.g. number.solaredge_multi_active_power_limit).
data_description.inverter_nominal_w: Maximum rated output of your inverter. SE3K = 3000, SE4K = 4000, SE5K = 5000. Check the label on your inverter.
data_description.grid_import_entity: ⬇️ Power you are drawing FROM the grid right now (W). In the DSMR/P1 integration this is 'Power Consumption' or 'electricity_meter_power_consumption'. Positive when consuming from the grid.
data_description.grid_export_entity: ⬆️ Power you are feeding BACK to the grid right now (W). In the DSMR/P1 integration this is 'Power Production' or 'electricity_meter_power_production'. Positive when exporting. Do NOT confuse with the inverter AC power sensor.
data_description.grid_voltage_entity: Optional: phase L1 voltage (V) from SolarEdge Modbus Multi. Only needed for over-voltage protection.

settings.title: Setpoints & parameters — step 2 of 2
settings.description: Set the maximum allowed export per mode and tune the control loop.

data_description.setpoint_normal_w: Maximum grid export in Normal mode. 50 W = small buffer to avoid importing. 0 W = aim for net-zero. 200 W = allow generous export.
data_description.setpoint_vacation_w: Used in Vacation mode. Typically 0 W so no surplus is exported while away.
data_description.setpoint_negative_price_w: Used when electricity price drops below the negative threshold. Typically 0 W to avoid paying to export.
data_description.setpoint_wide_w: Used in Wide mode. Higher value (e.g. 200 W) when some export is acceptable.
data_description.hysteresis_pct: Minimum change (%) before a new limit is written to the inverter. Prevents excessive Modbus writes. Default 1.5% works well.
data_description.update_interval_s: How often the control loop runs. 10 s recommended. Do not go below 5 s.
data_description.smoothing_window_s: Rolling average window for P1 sensor noise rejection. 8 s works well for most P1 meters.

recalculate.name: Recalculate now
recalculate.description: Force an immediate recompute and write.

reset_to_100.name: Reset limit to 100%
reset_to_100.description: Sets the SolarEdge active power limit to 100% (no curtailment).

set_mode.name: Set operating mode
set_mode.description: Switch the operating mode for all configured entries.
